name: Armenia
avoid: Icy mountain roads and fog in deep winter
Long southbound road transfers without an early start

cities.name: Yerevan
cities.notes: Republic Square, Cascade, pink-tuff architecture, markets and Armenia’s best food scene

cities.name: Dilijan
cities.notes: Forest-town base for Haghartsin, craft streets, easy hikes and cooler weather

cities.name: Tatev
cities.notes: Dramatic monastery reached by the Wings of Tatev cable car above the Vorotan Gorge

cities.name: Garni
cities.notes: Pagan temple, gorge landscapes, lavash baking and the Geghard monastery side trip

cities.name: Lake Sevan
cities.notes: Big blue lake with Sevanavank views, fish grills, breezy summer swims and peninsula sunsets

itinerary.sim: Team Telecom, Viva, or Ucom — easiest to buy at Yerevan airport or Republic Square shops
apps: GG and Yandex Go (cheap taxis in Yerevan and for short regional hops)
Yandex Maps or Google Maps (best for marshrutka routing and walking)
Maps.me (offline support for monastery roads and gorge trails)
Hyur Service / local WhatsApp drivers (useful for southbound Tatev transfers)

Yerevan.name: Yerevan
Yerevan.note: Best arrival base for cash, SIM cards, museums, and the easiest onward marshrutka links.

activities.name: Republic Square, Northern Avenue and Opera House walk through central Yerevan
Cascade Complex escalators, sculpture terraces and Cafesjian grounds
Matenadaran manuscript museum
Vernissage craft market browsing for carpets, ceramics and silver
Swan Lake and evening fountain show back at Republic Square

meals: Lavash — classic Armenian breads, dolma, salads · ₹630–₹1,260 pp
Sherep — polished local mains by Republic Square · ₹1,050–₹1,890 pp
Dalan Art Gallery and Restaurant — good lunch stop with craft courtyard · ₹630–₹1,260 pp

days.theme: History Museum, Blue Mosque & Brandy Tasting

activities.name: History Museum of Armenia on Republic Square
Armenian Genocide Memorial and Museum
Blue Mosque courtyard and prayer hall visit
Ararat Brandy Factory classic tour and tasting
GUM Market dry-fruit and gata snack crawl

meals: Tavern Yerevan — khorovats, salads, live-music dinner vibe · ₹840–₹1,680 pp
Anteb — reliable Armenian-Syrian lunch option · ₹630–₹1,050 pp
Zeituna Home of Food — mezze-heavy dinner in a restored house · ₹840–₹1,470 pp

Garni.name: Garni
Garni.note: Usually done as a day trip from Yerevan, but staying overnight makes the gorge quieter by evening.

activities.name: Yerevan to Garni by marshrutka from Gai station or by taxi
activities.cost: ₹60–₹105 pp by marshrutka or ₹1,260–₹1,680 per taxi

activities.name: Garni Temple
Geghard Monastery
Symphony of Stones in Garni Gorge
Lavash baking session at Garni Tun

meals: Garni Tun — lavash, khorovats and village-style lunch sets · ₹420–₹945 pp
7 Qar — garden seating with grilled meats and salads · ₹525–₹1,050 pp
Geghard Restaurant — trout, herbs and lavash near the monastery road · ₹420–₹945 pp

Dilijan.name: Dilijan
Dilijan.note: A cool-weather forest break that works well after Yerevan before heading to Sevan.

days.theme: Old Dilijan, Haghartsin & National Park Slow Travel

activities.name: Sharambeyan Street walk through Old Dilijan craft houses
Haghartsin Monastery excursion by taxi or local transfer
Dilijan National Park trail or Parz Lake stop
activities.cost: Free to hike; ₹315–₹630 for optional boat ride

activities.name: Dilijan Museum of Folk Art

meals: Kchuch — traditional clay-pot Armenian dishes · ₹630–₹1,260 pp
Cafe #2 — homemade soups, pastries and coffee · ₹315–₹735 pp
Tufenkian Restaurant — polished regional dinner in Old Dilijan · ₹840–₹1,470 pp

Lake Sevan.name: Lake Sevan
Lake Sevan.note: Best in summer and early autumn when the peninsula is lively and fish restaurants are fully open.

days.theme: Sevan Peninsula, Monastery Views & Fish Lunch

activities.name: Sevanavank Monastery and peninsula viewpoint climb
Boat ride from the peninsula or Sevan shore
Sevan Writers' House exterior and viewpoint stop
Lake swim / beach time in warm months

meals: Ashot Erkat — classic Sevan trout and crayfish stop · ₹525–₹1,260 pp
Noy Land Resort Restaurant — lake-view Armenian menu · ₹630–₹1,260 pp
Tsaghkunq Chef House — destination meal if you have a driver · ₹1,050–₹2,100 pp

Tatev.name: Tatev
Tatev.note: Long transfer day, but the gorge scenery and cable car ride justify an overnight if you can spare it.

days.theme: Vorotan Gorge, Wings of Tatev & Monastery Views

activities.name: Yerevan to Halidzor/Goris by marshrutka plus short taxi, or by direct taxi
activities.cost: ₹840–₹1,890 pp by public transport mix or ₹8,400–₹12,600 per taxi

activities.name: Wings of Tatev cable car round trip
Tatev Monastery
Tatev viewpoint walks and Sts. Paul and Peter grounds
Optional Devil's Bridge detour by taxi

meals: Harsnadzor Restaurant — hearty Armenian dishes with gorge views · ₹630–₹1,260 pp
Old Tatev Restaurant — convenient monastery-area dinner stop · ₹525–₹1,050 pp
Tatev Hotel restaurant — simple soups, grills and breakfast spreads · ₹420–₹945 pp

connections.method: Gai-station marshrutka or quick taxi ride (40–50 mins)
connections.cost: ₹60–₹105 pp by marshrutka or ₹1,260–₹1,680 per taxi

connections.method: Return to Yerevan and continue by direct marshrutka, or hire a private transfer (3–3.5 hrs total)
Direct marshrutka or shared taxi from the north bus station (2–2.5 hrs)
Shared taxi or marshrutka via Sevan town (1–1.5 hrs)
Summer train or regular marshrutka back to the capital (1.5 hrs)
Best done via Yerevan with marshrutka + taxi connections, or by private taxi south
Marshrutka toward Goris/Kapan plus taxi from Halidzor, or direct taxi (5.5–6.5 hrs)
Cable car back to Halidzor, then taxi to Goris and onward marshrutka or shared taxi

extras: Khor Virap with Mount Ararat views
Noravank Monastery detour on the Tatev route
